title: tsv_table_v1 — generic header-keyed TSV table
description: Permissive passthrough schema for any TSV whose row shape the atlas does NOT yet need to constrain. The extractor (extract_tsv_passthrough_v1) reads the file with the first non-comment line as the header, then emits one object per data row with column-name keys. Use this schema when (a) the upstream column set is still evolving, (b) the atlas only reads a subset of columns by name, or (c) a layer is declared in advance of a stricter v2 schema. Promote a layer to its own typed schema once column names stabilise and the page-side reads exercise enough columns to validate.
_notes: Row-level schema. The whole-file shape is `{ rows: tsv_table_v1[] }` — see the extractor for the wrapper.
additionalProperties allows any column name; values may be string|number|integer|boolean|null. The extractor decides type-coercion (default: numeric parse with NA → null fallback).
Comment lines starting with '#' are skipped by the extractor; the first non-comment line is treated as the header.

TSV files referenced by tsv_table_v1 today: anova_K{K}_{cohort}.tsv, pairwise_wilcox/ttest, cluster_summary, summary, outliers_*, q_correlations / q_linear_models, merged_K{K}_{cohort}.tsv, *_all.tsv aggregates, cleaned_heterozygosity, cleaned_structure_labels, pruned81_samples, best_seed_metadata, single_boxplot_input_dataframe, length_class_bins, samples_master, per_chromosome_master, froh_per_sample_per_chrom.
